There is wealth of wisdom in the address of Samuel
Gompers, before the National Civic Federation urging
employers not to reduce wages at this time


The president of the American Federation of Labor de-
ced that he had been approached by Of money who
had declared that wages must come down.


'.And turned to these men,, said Gompers, 'and
asked them whether the soil of country is less fertile
the treasures beneath the soil less valuable mems minds
less active the laborng less Industrious Wherefore
asked Them is there reason necessity to thus attack
the American stanandard of our workingman?'


We make hard times, just have made our
prosperity, if determined to have them. If cut
off reduce the purchasing power Of the aborer will
cut off sales.


The wise and patriotic employer is the who real
lies that the foundations of countrys prosperity
today they year ago, and that it is
the verest folly to be frightened into hard times by the
cries of the New York wolves.


Now is the time to stand pat
